evidence.command: node --test tests/review-intelligence-skill.test.js tests/agent-contracts.test.js tests/sync-agents-copy.test.js tests/sync-agents-preflight.test.js tests/sync-agents-preflight-semantic.test.js tests/update.test.js tests/skill-audit.test.js

evidence.result: Audit returned ok=true; template and workspace SKILL.md plus all four review-intelligence references have status ok.

The bounded two-pass, evidence-first, future-state, owner-routing, proportionate-research, private-reasoning prohibition, four-profile progressive-disclosure, Draft 2020-12 schema, closed-object, bounds, findings/evidence, and five-axis delivery-assurance contracts match the approved requirements. All seven template/workspace artifact pairs are byte-identical.

All six skill files and the schema are uniquely registered in MANAGED_FILES; root guidance additions are identical and preserve the existing workflow. The scoped constants diff changes only MANAGED_FILES.

Each hook uses the approved agent/profile and mode, follows activation/mission and precedes the existing gate or handoff, handles exits 0/1/2 without suppressing exit 2, and provides a bounded non-gating fallback. Template/workspace agent copies are byte-identical and existing language, output, observability, approval, gate, and handoff contracts remain present.

The documented kernel budget is 33000 bytes. Product is 32779 bytes with a 780-byte hook; Sheldon is 32173 bytes with an 819-byte hook. Detailed procedure remains in the on-demand skill and references.
